1_single_model_logprobs.verdict: Too compressed — insufficient variance for meaningful analysis

2_multi_model_disagreement.verdict: All Qwen models agree (>99%) — same training data, same biases

3_temperature_sampling.method: qwen-plus at T=0.7, 5 runs per statement
3_temperature_sampling.verdict: 100% agreement even at high temperature — model is overconfident

4_lm_llm_stance_distance.method: |LM% stance - LLM stance| where Dovish=-1, Neutral=0, Hawkish=1
4_lm_llm_stance_distance.verdict: BEST MEASURE — meaningful variance, theoretically grounded

5_composite_uncertainty_index.verdict: Useful but dominated by binary disagreement component

key_findings.F1_stance_distance_car: coef=-0.0091, p=0.005 *** — disagreement → worse bank returns
key_findings.F3_sd_zlb_variance: coef=+0.000332, p=0.051 * — ZLB amplifies disagreement effect
key_findings.F4_sd_dovish_zlb_variance: coef=-0.000364, p=0.093 * — ZLB+Dovish disagreement reduces dispersion (QE clarity)
key_findings.F5_stated_conf_variance: r=-0.356, p<0.0001 *** — LLM confidence predicts cross-sectional dispersion
key_findings.F6_zlb_higher_variance: coef=0.000274, p=0.039 ** — ZLB → more bank return dispersion

upgrade_path.short_term: Use LM-LLM stance distance as primary uncertainty measure (already works)
upgrade_path.medium_term: Add FOMC minutes text for richer semantic analysis (available for all 216 meetings)
upgrade_path.long_term: Use truly different model families (GPT-4, Claude, Llama) for multi-model disagreement
upgrade_path.alternative: Use market-implied uncertainty (VIX, Fed Funds futures) as external validation
